message:
Refine PR cross-link examples

Update the PR workflow skills to use native GitHub PR reference examples and shorten the companion docs label to 'Docs PR' for terser footer guidance.

files.patch:
@@ -74,14 +74,14 @@ For Linear, use its magic words explicitly in that footer: - Use separate lines only when the relationship differs, for example one line for `resolves` and another for `refs`. - When there is a companion docs PR, add a plain link line such as - `🔗 Documentation PR: https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/261` to the same - footer block rather than creating a dedicated section. + `🔗 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261` to the same footer block rather than creating + a dedicated section. Example footer: ```markdown -🔗 Documentation PR: https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/261
+🔗 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261
✅ Resolves TNZ-150, TNZ-151
🔗 Refs TNZ-152

@@ -29,10 +29,10 @@ Handle the operational workflow around docs.tenzir.com changes. 5. File a `tenzir/docs` pull request from `.docs/`. 6. Cross-link the pull requests using one shared compact footer pattern: - In the docs PR, append a final `...` footer line such as - `🔗 Code PR: `. + `🔗 Code PR: tenzir/tenzir#6048`. - In the code PR, append or extend the final `...` footer with - `🔗 Documentation PR: `. - - Prefer that footer over dedicated `Code PR` or `Documentation PR` + `🔗 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261`. + - Prefer that footer over dedicated `Code PR` or `Docs PR` sections. If a footer already exists, add another `
`-separated line instead of creating a second footer. 7. Summarize what changed and note any follow-up work. @@ -54,15 +54,15 @@ Docs PR example: ```markdown -🔗 Code PR: https://github.com/tenzir/tenzir/pull/1234 +🔗 Code PR: tenzir/tenzir#1234 ``` Code PR example: ```markdown -🔗 Documentation PR: https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/261
+🔗 Docs PR: tenzir/docs#261
✅ Resolves TNZ-150, TNZ-151 ```
